description: Native plant promotion, pollinator-friendly landscaping, and habitat restoration regulations

questions.title: What regulations or ordinances promote the use of native plants in landscaping, public spaces, or development projects?
questions.question: What regulations or ordinances promote the use of native plants in landscaping, public spaces, or development projects?
questions.scoreInstructions: Higher scores for clear, current rules with enforcement or incentives; lower for vague mentions or no regulations.

questions.title: Are there specific protections for pollinators such as bees, butterflies, or nesting habitat in local rules or plans?
questions.question: Are there specific protections for pollinators such as bees, butterflies, or nesting habitat in local rules or plans?
questions.scoreInstructions: Score higher if protections are explicit, species-specific, and tied to habitat or pesticide limits; lower if absent.

questions.title: Do landscaping standards require or encourage pollinator-friendly and low-mow native planting in new or renovated sites?
questions.question: Do landscaping standards require or encourage pollinator-friendly and low-mow native planting in new or renovated sites?
questions.scoreInstructions: Higher scores for standards that are mandatory or strongly incentivized and include design details; lower for general encouragement only.

questions.title: Are there designated areas for habitat restoration, native meadows, or pollinator corridors in parks, rights-of-way, or public lands?
questions.question: Are there designated areas for habitat restoration, native meadows, or pollinator corridors in parks, rights-of-way, or public lands?
questions.scoreInstructions: Score higher when designated areas are mapped, managed, and protected; lower if only informal or proposed.

questions.title: What community projects currently promote native plants and pollinators, such as planting programs, garden grants, or restoration events?
questions.question: What community projects currently promote native plants and pollinators, such as planting programs, garden grants, or restoration events?
questions.scoreInstructions: Higher scores for active, recurring projects with measurable participation or acreage; lower for one-time or unclear efforts.

questions.title: Do maintenance practices support pollinators, such as reduced mowing, seasonal timing, pesticide limits, or leaving nesting habitat?
questions.question: Do maintenance practices support pollinators, such as reduced mowing, seasonal timing, pesticide limits, or leaving nesting habitat?
questions.scoreInstructions: Score higher for documented practices with implementation guidance; lower if maintenance may harm pollinators or is unspecified.

questions.title: Are there incentives, rebates, or technical assistance for residents or developers to install native and pollinator-friendly landscapes?
questions.question: Are there incentives, rebates, or technical assistance for residents or developers to install native and pollinator-friendly landscapes?
questions.scoreInstructions: Higher scores for funded, accessible programs with clear eligibility; lower for no support or only informal advice.

questions.title: Is there monitoring or reporting on native plant coverage, pollinator habitat, or pollinator population outcomes over time?
questions.question: Is there monitoring or reporting on native plant coverage, pollinator habitat, or pollinator population outcomes over time?
questions.scoreInstructions: Score higher for regular monitoring with published results and trends; lower if no tracking exists.
